zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Beecher於1525年出生在英國英格蘭肯特艾登布利其時，她的父親Nicholas Beecher30歲，母親Eunice Peck30歲。她於1539年與John Culle在英國諾福爾可特林頓 聖 約翰結婚。她去世於1597年9月14日在她的家鄉享年72歲，埋葬在英國肯特艾登布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Beecher于1525年出生在英国英格兰肯特艾登布利其时，她的父亲Nicholas Beecher30岁，母亲Eunice Peck30岁。她于1539年与John Culle在英国諾福爾可特林頓 聖 約翰结婚。她去世于1597年9月14日在她的家乡享年72岁，埋葬在英国肯特艾登布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Beecher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Kent): from Middle English becher, denoting one who lived by a beech-tree, from an agent derivative of Middle English beche ‘beech-tree’ (see Beach ), the -er suffix having a locative sense. This English name is also common in Ireland, particularly in Cork.
Americanized form of German Bücher (cognate with 1 above) (see Bucher ).

variants.name: Beach
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English beche ‘beech-tree’ (Old English bēce) or Middle English beche ‘stream’ (Old English bæce). Middle English beche may be from Old English bēce ‘beech’ or bæce ‘stream’, and without further evidence it is impossible to distinguish these in Middle English.
Americanized form of Alsatian and German Bitsch: habitational name from Bitche (German Bitsch) in Alsace and Lorraine, at the foot of the northern slope of the Vosges.
Americanized form of German (mainly Hesse) Bitsch: unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Beecher was born in 1525, in Edenbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Nicholas Beecher, was 30 and her mother, Eunice Peck, was 30. She married John Culle in 1539, in Terrington St John, Norfolk, England. She died on 14 September 1597, in her hometown, at the age of 72, and was buried in Edenbridge, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Beecher est née en 1525, à Edenbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Nicholas Beecher, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Eunice Peck, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Culle en 1539, à Terrington St John, Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 14 septembre 1597, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Edenbridge, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Beecher 1525 in Edenbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Beecher 30 und ihre Mutter Eunice Peck 30. Sie hat 1539 in Terrington St John, Norfolk, England John Culle geheiratet. Sie starb am 14. September 1597 im Alter von 72 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Edenbridge, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Beecher è nata il nel 1525 nel Edenbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Nicholas Beecher, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Eunice Peck, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato John Culle nel 1539, nel Terrington St John, Norfolk, England. Morì il 14 settembre 1597, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolta a Edenbridge, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Beecherが1525年にイギリスイギリスケントイーデンブリッジで生まれた時，父Nicholas Beecherは30歳，母Eunice Peckは30歳であった。1539年にイギリスノーフォルクテリントン スト ジョンでJohn Culleと結婚した。彼女は1597年9月14日に故郷で72歳で死去し，イギリスケントイーデンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Beecher(이)가 1525년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 에덴브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Beecher(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eunice Peck(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1539년 영국 노르폴크 테링턴 스트 존에서 John Culle(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1597년 9월 14일 그녀의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 에덴브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Beecher nasceu em 1525, em Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Nicholas Beecher, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Eunice Peck, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John Culle em 1539, em Terrington St John, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1597, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Beecher родилась в 1525 г. в местности эденбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Nicholas Beecher было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Eunice Peck, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Culle в 1539 г. в местности тиррингтон ст джон, норфолк, Англия. Она умерла 14 сентября 1597 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности эденбридж, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Beecher nació en 1525, en Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Nicholas Beecher, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Eunice Peck, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John Culle en 1539, en Terrington St John, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1597, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Edenbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.
